reason_of_clustering: Gold and silver are both classified as precious metals and often show similar behavioral patterns in response to market trends, inflation, and geopolitical tensions. Investors typically view them as safe-haven assets during times of economic uncertainty.

The Dollar Index measures the value of the U.S. dollar against a basket of currencies. Gold is often inversely correlated with the value of the dollar; when the dollar weakens, gold prices tend to rise as it becomes cheaper for investors using other currencies.

Gold and silver are common alternatives to traditional currency investments, while the Dollar Index serves as a benchmark for evaluating the strength of the dollar relative to other currencies. Analyzing these entities together provides investors with insights into asset diversification strategies.
